key_insight: RNC-LM fixes a key weakness of Levenberg-Marquardt by using Riemann normal coordinates to make finite optimization steps more geometrically consistent and reduce parameterization-induced curvature effects.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more robust nonlinear least-squares solver for tasks like PINNs, regression, and potential-energy-surface fitting where curved valleys and bad parameterizations can slow or destabilize training.
method: Reformulates the geodesic update in a moving tangent frame.
Adds higher-order corrections beyond standard geodesic acceleration for finite steps.
Uses a line search along the RNC curve to control step length while preserving LM-like cost reduction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved convergence on classical nonlinear least-squares benchmarks, reduced PINN error to about 1e-3 on a reaction-diffusion failure case, and a 34-fold speedup on a large-scale potential-energy-surface fitting task.
watch_item: Higher-order geometric updates add algorithmic complexity, so practical performance may depend on how the RNC expansion, line search, and stopping criteria are implemented.
paper_title: Higher-Order Geometric Updates for Levenberg-Marquardt Method via Riemann Normal Coordinates
